conjecture_title: Open problem: dimension of posets with planar cover graphs vs. height
paper_title: Planar posets have dimension at most linear in their height
summary: The conjecture asks whether the dimension of posets with planar cover graphs is bounded by a polynomial (or linear) function of their height. Kozik, Micek, and Trotter (arXiv:1907.00380, 2019) resolved the polynomial part affirmatively by proving an O(h^6) upper bound; Gorsky and Seweryn (arXiv:2103.15920, 2021) subsequently improved this to O(h^3). Whether a linear bound holds—analogous to the main theorem of the source paper for genuinely planar posets—remains open.

since_posted.title: Dimension is polynomial in height for posets with planar cover graphs
since_posted.authors: Jakub Kozik, Piotr Micek, William T. Trotter
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves that height-h posets with planar cover graphs have dimension O(h^6), establishing the first polynomial upper bound and resolving the polynomial-existence part of the conjecture.

since_posted.title: Posets with k-outerplanar cover graphs have bounded dimension
since_posted.claim: As a secondary result, improves the polynomial upper bound for posets with planar cover graphs from O(h^6) to O(h^3); the primary result establishes that k-outerplanar cover graphs force dimension O(k^3).

search_queries: planar cover graph poset dimension height polynomial bound Joret Micek Wiechert
dimension posets planar cover graphs linear polynomial height 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The polynomial-existence part of the conjecture is resolved: dimension is O(h^3) for height-h posets with planar cover graphs (Gorsky-Seweryn 2021). The linear-bound part remains open. Planarity is essential: there exist posets with exponential dimension in height whose cover graphs exclude K_5 as a minor, showing minor-freeness alone is insufficient for polynomial bounds.
